title: Subscription Plan Labels — show 'Most Popular' and 'Best Value' badges on plan cards
commit_message: feat: add PLAN_LABELS constant and getPlanLabel helper — render colored badge on Monthly ('Most Popular') and Yearly ('Best Value') plan cards
changed_symbols: PLAN_LABELS (new export — { Monthly: 'Most Popular', Yearly: 'Best Value' })
getPlanLabel (new export — returns PLAN_LABELS[billingPeriod] or null)
SubscriptionPlans render (modified — badge View rendered above each plan card when getPlanLabel returns a truthy value)

what_the_diff_does.before: Subscription plan cards show only price and billing period. There is no visual indicator distinguishing which plan is recommended.
what_the_diff_does.after: Each plan card shows a small colored badge at the top: 'Most Popular' for the Monthly plan and 'Best Value' for the Yearly plan. Badges use the app's red theme color and white text.
what_the_diff_does.visible_change: Users see a 'Most Popular' red badge on the Monthly plan card and a 'Best Value' red badge on the Yearly plan card when viewing the Subscription Plans screen.
what_the_diff_does.compile_note: PLAN_LABELS and getPlanLabel are added as module-level exports before the SubscriptionPlans component — no existing imports or props are changed.

CRITICAL.reason: subscription-labels.pure.test.js declares SubscriptionPlans.js as @source and covers every new symbol: PLAN_LABELS (5 tests), getPlanLabel (9 tests). All 14 are CRITICAL.
tests: PLAN_LABELS > Monthly and Yearly labels are distinct strings
PLAN_LABELS > both label strings are non-empty
getPlanLabel > returns null for an unknown billing period
getPlanLabel > returns null for empty string
getPlanLabel > Monthly plan has a truthy label so the badge always renders
getPlanLabel > Yearly plan has a truthy label so the badge always renders

HIGH.reason: payment-state.cross.test.js covers the full payment/subscription state machine, including SubscriptionPlan API calls. These tests must pass to confirm badge labels don't interfere with subscription data flow.

SKIP.reason: All remaining test files do not declare SubscriptionPlans.js as a source and do not test plan label logic.

coverage_gaps.gap: No existing tests covered this new constant.
No existing tests covered this new helper function.

jest_run_note: subscription-labels.pure.test.js runs 14 CRITICAL tests for PLAN_LABELS and getPlanLabel. payment-state.cross.test.js runs 22 HIGH tests confirming subscription data flow is unaffected.
